title: Bump to v5.4.0-RC1
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Second try, this time including Paul's vendor updates

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: This is twice in a row I forgot to CI:ALL the rc1 PR. I wonder if we can check for individual commit diffs to rawversion...

> This is twice in a row I forgot to CI:ALL the rc1 PR. I wonder if we can check for individual commit diffs to rawversion...

We have no way to do that, cirrus checks what sources are changed on total diff. Just like any other tool I am aware of. The openshift bot is also broken as `/approve` doesn't work on these. So I guess the overall thing should be not do empty PRs?

We can try to special case the PR name in the cirrus.yml to run everything if you are consistent on that.
Once releases are automated, the bump to `-dev` could be automatic - that'd remove the final commit and make this a non-empty diff

> Once releases are automated, the bump to `-dev` could be automatic - that'd remove the final commit and make this a non-empty diff

Right yeah, with that new flow it should no longer be a problem so not worth to try to look into fixing the issue

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** Approval requirements bypassed by manually added approval. This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *lsm5*, *Luap99*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
